title: HTTP Semantics, Resources, Methods, Preconditions, and Status · Independent Type Trial
summary: An independent service team receives a different tenant, traffic, trust, persistence, and failure profile and transfers http semantics, resources, methods, preconditions, and status into a new resource, method, status, validator, and retry contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of resource, method, status, validator, and retry contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Separate resource identity, server state, selected representation, transfer content, TypeScript model, and PostgreSQL rows.
Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method properties independently and preserve them through application effects.
Choose POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, status, Location, representation, and asynchronous completion behavior from the resource contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Resource Representation Storage
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 9384 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to connect method semantics, resource state, preconditions, status, and durable effect; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then emit a server error and retain one low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate resource identity, server state, selected representation, transfer content, TypeScript model, and PostgreSQL rows.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Resource Representation Storage.

hints: For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Semantics Retry Proof, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Semantics Retry Proof precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Semantics Retry Proof, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab team handles case 2220 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to connect method semantics, resource state, preconditions, status, and durable effect; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then change the tenant and prove denial before repository access. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test methods against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, changed state, and durable effects.

For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Semantics Retry Proof, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: One expected status assertion proves an endpoint obeys HTTP semantics.

stimulus.title: Semantics Retry Proof failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: One expected status assertion proves an endpoint obeys HTTP semantics.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test methods against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, changed state, and durable effects.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Semantics Retry Proof condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Semantics Retry Proof happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Semantics Retry Proof
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Semantics Retry Proof: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab team handles case 9601 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to connect method semantics, resource state, preconditions, status, and durable effect; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then abort during a dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test methods against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, changed state, and durable effects.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Semantics Retry Proof.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Semantics Retry Proof, keep Semantics Retry Proof connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test methods against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, changed state, and durable effects.
